It is hard, but I have been keeping up better than I expected.

I want to tell you about something I have been thinking about lately: my phone. For the last few years, I have been spending more and more time on screens. Every morning, I wake up and the first thing I do is check my notifications. I have been relying on my phone for everything, from work emails to the news. The problem is, I have been getting distracted very easily. I sit down to do one task, and an hour later I am still scrolling. Last month I noticed my screen time was almost six hours a day. I have been trying to change this habit. I have been turning off my notifications in the evening, and I have been keeping my phone in another room while I work. It has not been easy, and I am still struggling with it, but slowly things are getting better. The truth is, technology is wonderful, but we have to stay in control of it, not the other way around.

Let me tell you what has been happening with my devices this week. My phone has been asking me to install an update for three days now, and I have been ignoring it. I know I should do it, because an update keeps the device safe, but I have been struggling to find the time. Meanwhile, my old laptop has been running very slowly, and I have been relying on my phone for almost everything. My granddaughter has been helping me. She has been showing me how to manage my notifications so I do not get distracted all day. She told me something interesting: she has been keeping up with all her friends through one single app. I have been learning a lot from her. For someone my age, technology can feel difficult, but I have been keeping up better than I expected. Little by little, I am becoming more confident with my screen and my devices.

First, look at how much screen time you have had this week. Then turn off the notifications you do not need, so you get distracted less. Next, choose one app to keep up with the people who matter most. After that, install any update your device has been asking for. Finally, keep your phone in another room while you work, and notice how much calmer you feel.
